mnemonics.title: PRNG Security Properties
mnemonics.content:
FORWARD: Forget Old Results, Want No Yields
BACKWARD: Breaking A Current State Doesn't Yield Future Outputs
(Forward = past secure, Backward = future secure)

mnemonics.title: BBS Setup Requirements
mnemonics.content:
P must be PROUD: Prime ≡ 3 (mod 4)
Both p, q must satisfy this for quadratic residue computation

mnemonics.title: CSPRNG Requirements
mnemonics.content: SPEAR: Statistical test Pass, Entropy adequate, Attack resistant, Random-looking output

acronyms.usage: Simple PRNG with recurrence X(n+1) = (aX(n) + c) mod m
Cryptographically secure PRNG based on integer factorization

acronyms.expansion: Counter Mode Deterministic Random Bit Generator
acronyms.usage: NIST standardized PRNG using block cipher in counter mode

acronyms.expansion: National Institute Standards and Technology Special Publication 800-90A
acronyms.usage: Standard for DRBGs: Hash, HMAC, CTR, and Dual EC methods

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowing pipeline: Seed → [State Update with one-way function] → Output → Next State. Arrow into State should be 'broken' to show forward security (can't go back).
LCG: small box with simple + and × operations. BBS: large box with nested circles representing prime factorization trapdoor.
A mixing basin with multiple taps (mouse, keyboard, timing) pouring in, with output valve at bottom. Shows entropy mixing before output.

analogies.analogy: Like the starting position on a board game - where you start determines the entire game path, but the path looks random to observers.
Like a one-way mirror - once light (output) passes through, you can't retrieve it from the other side even if you have the current glass (state).
Like having a combination lock (p and q) - easy to multiply numbers together, but extremely hard to unmultiply and find the original factors.
Like gathering rainwater from multiple gutters - unpredictable drips accumulate into a pool, and mixing makes it hard to trace any drop's origin.
